{
  "text": "Three years ago, the ratio of Priya's age to Qasim's age was 2:5. In 5 years' time, the ratio of their ages will be 4:7. How old is Qasim now?",
  "options": ["13", "15", "17", "18", "20"],
  "correctIndex": 2,
  "solution": "Let Priya's current age be P and Qasim's current age be Q.

Three years ago: (P - 3) : (Q - 3) = 2 : 5
So 5(P - 3) = 2(Q - 3)
5P - 15 = 2Q - 6
5P = 2Q + 9 ... (1)

In 5 years' time: (P + 5) : (Q + 5) = 4 : 7
So 7(P + 5) = 4(Q + 5)
7P + 35 = 4Q + 20
7P = 4Q - 15 ... (2)

From (1): P = (2Q + 9) / 5
Substitute into (2): 7 × (2Q + 9) / 5 = 4Q - 15
(14Q + 63) / 5 = 4Q - 15
14Q + 63 = 20Q - 75
63 + 75 = 20Q - 14Q
138 = 6Q
Q = 23... Let me recalculate.

Actually let me redo with different numbers that work cleanly.

Let me try: Three years ago ratio 2:5, in 5 years ratio 4:7.

5(P-3) = 2(Q-3) → 5P - 15 = 2Q - 6 → 5P = 2Q + 9
7(P+5) = 4(Q+5) → 7P + 35 = 4Q + 20 → 7P = 4Q - 15

From first: P = (2Q+9)/5
7(2Q+9)/5 = 4Q - 15
(14Q + 63)/5 = 4Q - 15
14Q + 63 = 20Q - 75
138 = 6Q
Q = 23

That gives Q = 23 which isn't in my options. Let me redesign.

New approach: Two years ago, the ratio of Priya's age to Qasim's age was 1:3. In 6 years' time, the ratio will be 3:5. Find Qasim's current age.

3(P-2) = 1(Q-2) → 3P - 6 = Q - 2 → Q = 3P - 4 ... (1)
5(P+6) = 3(Q+6) → 5P + 30 = 3Q + 18 → 5P = 3Q - 12 ... (2)

Substitute (1) into (2): 5P = 3(3P-4) - 12 = 9P - 12 - 12 = 9P - 24
5P - 9P = -24
-4P = -24
P = 6
Q = 3(6) - 4 = 14

Check: Two years ago: 4:12 = 1:3 ✓
In 6 years: 12:20 = 3:5 ✓

Q = 14. Let me adjust my options. Actually let me rewrite the whole question with these numbers."
}

Wait, let me redo this properly.

{"text":"Two years ago, the ratio of Priya's age to Qasim's age was 1:3. In 6 years' time, the ratio of their ages will be 3:5. How old is Qasim now?","options":["10","12","14","16","18"],"correctIndex":2,"solution":"Let Priya's current age be P and Qasim's current age be Q.

Two years ago their ages were (P−2) and (Q−2), and the ratio was 1:3.
So Q−2 = 3(P−2), which gives Q−2 = 3P−6, so Q = 3P−4 ... (1)

In 6 years their ages will be (P+6) and (Q+6), and the ratio will be 3:5.
So 5(P+6) = 3(Q+6), which gives 5P+30 = 3Q+18, so 5P = 3Q−12 ... (2)

Substitute (1) into (2):
5P = 3(3P−4)−12
5P = 9P−12−12
5P = 9P−24
24 = 4P
P = 6

From (1): Q = 3(6)−4 = 14

Check: Two years ago: 4 and 12 → ratio 1:3 ✓
In 6 years: 12 and 20 → ratio 3:5 ✓

Qasim is 14 years old."}
